payload.author: agent
payload.body:
Delivered. Cross-crate named-arg enforcement now works against a signature index auto-extracted from ANY crate — no hand-written trust-std-style shim.

Engine (crates/trust-lower/src/sig_index.rs, plain Rust, 15 new tests, clippy-clean):
- extract_from_source / extract_from_dir: pub-fn-only, ambiguity-aware extraction (syn, with lower()-fallback for files using Trust syntax).
- render_manifest / parse_manifest: name:p1,p2 format shared with trust-std/std-signatures.txt (runtime parser is lenient; build.rs stays strict).
- merge: union of indices, drops conflicting names (degrades to positional fallback, never a wrong reorder).
- load_paths / load_from_env: reads TRUST_SIGNATURE_PATH (platform-split list of manifests/dirs).

Wiring:
- trust index <src|file|-> [-o file]  (new CLI subcommand) — auto-extracts a crate's public API.
- trust check/lower/build + the trust-rustc wrapper seed the CalleeRegistry from TRUST_SIGNATURE_PATH; the wrapper merges it with the crate-wide extras.

Proof (acceptance criterion met): a positional swap into a third-party fn (producer::make_rect(1080,1920) where params are width,height) is SILENTLY ACCEPTED with no index and REJECTED with R0042 once the auto-extracted index is loaded; a named cross-crate call reorders to declared order and a full cargo build through the wrapper compiles+runs (1920*1080=2073600). Checked-in fixture: examples/cross-crate-index/ + CI step + 15 unit tests.

Remaining (NOT in this story's acceptance): zero-config discovery of dependency indices from cargo's build graph — you still generate indices and name them via the env var. Docs (WHY/SPEC/README/AGENTS) updated to reflect this honestly.
